title: gypsophila repens var. alpestris
intro: Gypsophila repens var. alpestris, also known as Alpine Baby's Breath, is a low-growing, perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its delicate white flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain and swelling, and calm digestive issues. The health benefits of Gypsophila repens var. alpestris include its ability to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress relief and overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Gypsophila repens var. alpestris include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties and therapeutic benefits.
